chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Obligation of Returning a Bequest to What is Proper and What the Deceased is Entitled to From His Wealth

text: 5425 - رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي رَجُلٍ تُوُفِّيَ وَ أَوْصَى بِمَالِهِ كُلِّهِ أَوْ بِأَكْثَرِهِ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْوَصِيَّةَ تُرَدُّ إِلَى اَلْمَعْرُوفِ وَ يُتْرَكُ لِأَهْلِ اَلْمِيرَاثِ مِيرَاثُهُمْ" ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5425 - Aṣim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding a man who passed away and had bequeathed all of his wealth or most of it.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: 'The will is to be adjusted to what is customary, and the rightful inheritance must be left for the heirs.'"
